message:
feat(mux): add DirectMuxClient connection lifecycle tracing with connection IDs

Add comprehensive structured tracing to the vendored DirectMuxClient to enable
production debugging of mux protocol issues without increasing log volume at
default levels (most spans are trace-level):

- Assign monotonic connection_id to each DirectMuxClient instance via
  AtomicU64 counter, included in all tracing spans for correlation
- Add debug-level spans at connect entry (showing configured vs resolved
  compression mode) and on successful connection (showing full config)
- Add warn-level spans on handshake failures (codec_version, register_client),
  compression fallback retries, write errors, read errors, frame-too-large
  violations, and duplicate response serial observations
- Add trace-level spans for request encoding, write completion, batch
  start/complete, response decode, out-of-order response stashing, and
  pending-map cache hits
- Replace bare `?` operators on write/read with explicit match arms that
  log before propagating errors, distinguishing IO errors from timeouts
- Switch test import from tokio::sync::Mutex to runtime_compat::Mutex for
  consistency with the async runtime abstraction layer

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
